text.title: ADR Decay Detector Agent Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules with rationale + source for kb-adr-decay-detector-agent. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Detector MUST scan every ADR in `docs/adr/` (or configured root); selective scans miss decay.
rule.rationale: ADR decay is non-local; a contradiction can appear far from the ADR location.

rule.statement: Contradiction findings MUST cite both the ADR section and the conflicting code (file + line); free-form 'this seems wrong' rejected.
rule.rationale: Evidence-anchored findings hold up in review; non-evidenced findings get dismissed and erode trust.

rule.statement: Supersession (`replaces:` / `superseded-by:`) MUST be a markdown link to the superseding ADR; orphan supersedes flagged.
rule.rationale: Without link the graph rots; readers can't navigate decisions.

rule.statement: ADRs referencing files / modules / libraries that no longer exist MUST be flagged with the broken path.
rule.rationale: Dead references signal decisions ground-truth has shifted.

rule.statement: ADRs with status `accepted` but no implementation evidence (file path, PR, deploy) MUST be flagged.
rule.rationale: `accepted` without evidence is aspirational, not decision-of-record.

rule.statement: Detector MUST run weekly (or on schedule per team); ad-hoc runs miss steady-state decay.
rule.rationale: Weekly cadence catches drift before next quarter's review.

rule.statement: Findings MUST be routed to the architecture review queue (Linear ticket / Jira issue / GitHub issue); console-only output is ignored.
rule.rationale: Discoverability is the killer of decay detection.

rule.statement: If <5 ADRs exist or ADRs are pure prose, return `skipped`.
